eyebrow: If I Awaken in Los Angeles · Lounge & Front of House
title: The Lounge
summary: A two-part front-of-house experience. The audience first arrives in an outdoor courtyard with a Hollywood-style red carpet check-in under the LA sky, then steps through a single threshold door into a warm indoor club lounge with the bar, gift shop, and entrance to the show. Same ~9,500 sq ft of front-of-house, but split between sky and shelter.
callout:
The lounge is split by an existing wall into two halves. The outdoor courtyard (~3,800 sq ft) sits at the north end — gated off the parking lot at its narrow tip, with the warehouse exterior wall on the west and a chamfered fence on the east. This is where the red carpet, the step-and-repeat backdrop, the photographer, and the ticket scan all live, in the open air. LA's weather makes this viable most of the year; a retractable canopy handles rain.

The indoor lounge (~5,700 sq ft) is the larger half, accessed through a single door in the middle of the green/blue wall. Inside: a ~64-ft bar, cocktail tables, a 5-booth VIP section along the angled wall, a curated gift shop split into north and south sections, and the show entry/exit doorway in the middle of the west wall (shared with Space 1 / Space 7). The aesthetic principle holds across both halves — Old Hollywood premiere glamour layered onto Arts District industrial — but expressed differently: the courtyard is sky-lit and palm-lined, the indoor is brick-and-velvet warm.

Pre-show, the audience flows parking → gate → red carpet → indoor door → bar → show. Post-show, they reverse: show → gift shop → optional bar → indoor door → courtyard → gate → parking. The single threshold door between the two zones means everyone passes through both spaces twice — once before the show, once after.

meta.label: Street gate
meta.value: North end of green · 21.7 ft wide

          <!-- ============== ZONE 2: BAR / WAITING (rest of indoor · BAR LENGTH RESIZABLE) ============== -->

legend.label: Outdoor courtyard (green)
legend.note: 3,800 sq ft · open-air with paver flooring · paths, palms, string lights

legend.label: Red carpet
legend.note: ~75 ft along west wall · backdrop on warehouse exterior · velvet ropes on east side

legend.label: Street gate (top of green)
legend.note: 21.7 ft wide iron gate + marquee · primary public entry

legend.label: Indoor lounge (blue)
legend.note: 5,700 sq ft · bar, gift shop, show doorway, restrooms

legend.label: Gift shop
legend.note: Wraps the show doorway · post-show audience emerges into retail

beats.title: Arrival at the gate
beats.body: Audience approaches the venue from the parking lot. The exterior is otherwise unremarkable warehouse — but the iron gate, with its marquee overhead, is unmistakable. The marquee reads If I Awaken in Los Angeles, frosted bulbs lit. A host stationed at the gate scans tickets and welcomes guests. They pass through the gate into an outdoor courtyard, golden-hour lit, palm-lined, with string lights overhead.

tags.label: Marquee bulbs · warm uplight on iron gate
Distant indoor music + outdoor ambient
Guests arrive from parking · approach gate

beats.title: Red carpet check-in (outdoor)
beats.body: Once through the gate, audience steps onto the red carpet runway. Halfway down, they reach the step-and-repeat backdrop on the warehouse exterior wall. A host in character welcomes each guest or group: "Welcome to the premiere — step on the star, look this way." Photographer captures the photo. The premiere conceit lands fully — the audience is treated as if they are the celebrity arrivals, and tonight, in this courtyard, they are.

tags.label: Theatrical front lights · backdrop spot
Old Hollywood standards spilling from indoor
Onto carpet · pose · click
Host in character · photographer

beats.title: Through the door, into the lounge
beats.body: Audience continues down the rest of the carpet and approaches the door at the far end of the courtyard — the threshold between outdoor and indoor. As they cross, they leave the open sky and step into a warm, brick-lined club. The music swells. The bar comes into view. Coat check is right inside the door for those who need it. The transition itself is theatrical: premiere on the carpet, party in the lounge.

tags.label: Door warm-glowing · indoor music spills out
Music swells as door opens
Through the door · coat check just inside

beats.title: Settling into the bar
beats.body: Audience moves to the 50-ft bar or finds a cocktail table. Bartenders work the rail; servers move through with cocktails for tables. The walls catch the eye — vintage neon signs from real LA businesses, B&W photos of neighborhoods that aren't Hollywood. The cocktail menu names places: Boyle Heights Sunset, The Topanga, Crenshaw & Slauson. Audience asks each other: "Have you been there?" The show's argument has already begun, quietly.

tags.label: Warm bar wash · neon glow · candles
Curated jazz · conversational volume
Free movement · order drinks · find seats

beats.title: 5-min call · into the show
beats.body: Music dips for a moment. A single chime. Floor managers move through the bar, quietly directing audience toward the show entry doorway in the middle of the west wall. Audience finishes drinks and walks the short distance to the door. They pass briefly through the gift shop area — foreshadowing — and step through into Space 1. The show begins.

tags.label: Bar lights pulse soft · door warms
Music dips · single chime
Walk to show doorway · west wall
Floor managers usher

beats.title: Emerging from Space 7 · into the walking path
beats.body: Audience exits the Space 7 corridor through the same west-wall doorway they entered through 100 minutes ago. They emerge into the walking path — a corridor flanked on both sides by gift shop sections. Gift Shop North on their left (with the photo-print kiosk and show merchandise); Gift Shop South on their right (with LA-made goods, books, and the cashier). The first thing they see after the Living Mosaic is shelves of LA-made goods on both sides. The show's argument continues physically: "the city is its people; here are some of them."

tags.label: Gift shop warm on both sides · gallery lighting
Quiet ambient · band's last chord nearly gone
Through the doorway · into the corridor between shops

beats.title: Browsing the city · both shops
beats.body: Audience moves through both shops at their own pace. In the north section, some line up at the photo-print kiosk to print their best frame from the show; others browse show merchandise — vinyl, posters, programs. In the south section, some pause at the LA neighborhood feature wall and pick up books — Octavia Butler, Joan Didion, a poetry collection by an LA poet they didn't know existed; others gather candles, coffee, ceramics from LA businesses. Many will visit both. The corridor between makes flow easy: browse north, cross to south, head east toward the bar or the door.

tags.label: Soft directional spots · warm wall wash
Quiet · bar music bleeds in faintly
North shop · south shop · linger or pass

beats.title: Back to the bar (optional)
beats.body: Roughly half the audience drifts back into the bar for a post-show drink. The room reads differently now — the cocktail named The Sekou means something. The neon sign reading Los Angeles means something. Conversations are about the show, the moments, the lines they remember. The bartenders, who've been serving all night, become listeners. The other half of the audience continues toward the exit.

tags.label: Bar warm · same as pre-show
Same playlist returns · slightly quieter
Some return to bar · others head outside

beats.title: Through the door · back into the courtyard
beats.body: Audience passes through the indoor entry door — but in reverse. Coats retrieved. They step back into the outdoor courtyard, now with string lights as the primary illumination, palms swaying, the city's night air. Some linger here for a final conversation under the sky. Others walk the carpet back toward the gate.

tags.label: Outdoor cooler · string lights still on
Indoor music fades behind · LA night ambient
Through indoor door · into courtyard

beats.title: Through the gate · back to the city
beats.body: Audience exits through the iron gate they entered through. Behind them, the marquee still glows. Ahead of them: the parking lot, the Arts District street, the 6th Street viaduct in the distance, downtown skyline. The show's argument lands one more time, in real space: this is the city the show was about. You are walking in it now. The closing image of the show is not on a wall — it is the city itself, outside the gate.

tags.label: Marquee bulbs · streetlight beyond
Distant traffic · the actual city
Through the gate · into the parking lot

locked: Lounge is split into two halves: outdoor courtyard (green, ~3,800 sq ft) at the north end, indoor lounge (blue, ~5,700 sq ft) at the south end
Single threshold door connects them, centered in the green/blue wall
Street/parking entry: 21.7-ft-wide iron gate at the top of the green courtyard, with marquee overhead
Show entry/exit doorway in the middle of the indoor lounge's west wall (= middle of Space 1's east wall)
Loop flow: pre-show parking → gate → red carpet → indoor → bar → show; post-show show → gift shop (north or south) → bar (optional) → indoor door → courtyard → gate → parking
Red carpet runway lives entirely outdoors, ~75 ft long along the warehouse exterior west wall
Step-and-repeat backdrop mounted on the warehouse exterior wall (west wall of courtyard), 12 ft × 30 ft
Photo capture moment integrated into outdoor red carpet check-in (feeds the Space 7 Living Mosaic system)
Ticket scan station positioned mid-courtyard adjacent to red carpet
Souvenir card with QR code given at gate scan or post-photo (gates personalized photo gallery delivery)

~64-ft bar shifted east toward the angled wall, with bartenders, a 5-booth VIP section along the chamfered NE wall (velvet rope perimeter, dedicated host stand), and cocktail tables clustered around it
Cocktail menu named after show moments and LA neighborhoods
Music: jazz/Latin jazz/soul/R&B pre-show; same playlist returns post-show at lower volume; spills from indoor to outdoor
Bar walls: existing brick exposed, layered with vintage LA neon signs and B&W photos of real LA neighborhoods
Gift shop split into TWO sections — North (~648 sq ft, 27 × 24 ft) and South (~700 sq ft, 28 × 25 ft) — with a ~17-ft walking corridor between them aligned with the show entry/exit doorway
North section: photo-print kiosk + show merchandise (first encountered post-show)
South section: books by LA voices + goods from LA businesses + cashier + LA neighborhood feature wall
Walking corridor preserves direct east-west flow from bar/lounge to show entry; never blocked by retail

Aesthetic principle: Old Hollywood premiere glamour layered onto raw Arts District industrial; never disguise the warehouse beneath; outdoor courtyard expresses this with palms+iron+marquee, indoor with brick+velvet+neon
Coat check just inside the indoor entry door (shifted slightly east of door)
Restrooms use existing infrastructure on the south side of the indoor lounge
3 palm trees positioned in the outdoor courtyard along the eastern fence side
String lights overhead in the outdoor courtyard
Standing audience and bar table positions tested across 150-pax capacity
stillOpen: Weather contingency mechanism — retractable canopy, tensile shade, pop-up tent, or move indoors entirely on rain days
Iron gate detailing — sliding vs swinging vs double-leaf · marquee typography
Outdoor lighting plot — string light layout, uplighting on warehouse exterior, ground accent
Outdoor flooring — existing concrete vs new pavers vs polished concrete
Outdoor planting palette — palms, jacarandas, succulents, native plants (LA-specific designer)
Outdoor cocktail tables — quantity, placement, weather-resistant materials
Step-and-repeat key art (graphic design)
